title: SIGNIFICANCE OF EKADASHI | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: A warm welcome to all of you for today's Geeta Live session.
Today we are going to talk about Ekadashi Vrata.
What is the significance of this Vrata?
Why people fast on Ekadashi?
What is the significance of this fasting?
So we will talk about all these things in detail.
Before we start our discussion, we will start our session with prayers.
Request all of you to kindly fold your hands.
And join me in offering these prayers so that we can have the blessings of Prabhupad, Lord
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Lord Shri Krishna and with their blessings we get into this discussion.
Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Prishtaya Bhutale
Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine
Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharinee
Nirvisesha Sunyavadi Pasc希 Atya Deshatarinee
Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda
Shri Advait Gadadhara

before i talk about ekadashi vrata or fasting on the ekadashi days let me in general talk

why do we fast what is the significance of fasting in general we fast on some important

We all end up spending a lot of time on our bodily needs, our sensual needs.
As we have been discussing, we have five senses, eyes, ears, nose, tongue and touch.
And each of these senses are constantly looking for sense objects, for gratification, for enjoyment.
For example, tongue is always looking for nice palatable eatables.
Eyes are looking for beautiful forms, something which is pleasing for the eyes.
And we all end up spending a lot of time gratifying the needs of our senses, gratifying the needs of our body.
In other words, maintaining this body.
And in the process, we end up not spending sufficient time to nourish the soul.
If you see an average person, 5 minutes, 10 minutes, 15 minutes he spends doing some pujas, doing some prayers, doing some worship.
But throughout the day, all the activities is all pertaining to fulfilling the bodily needs.
So, on fasting days, the idea, the overall understanding is that how we can minimize the requirements, the time we spend for our bodily needs, and focus more on nourishing the soul.
So, on fasting days, the best thing to do is minimize our bodily needs, spend less time in eating, sleeping.
So, on fasting days, the best thing to do is minimize our bodily needs, spend less time in eating, sleeping.
So, on fasting days, the best thing to do is minimize our bodily needs, spend less time in eating, sleeping.

Like chanting, reading about Krishna, reading Bhagavad Gita, reading Srimad Bhagavatam, reading Krishna book, hearing about the Lord, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu Smaranam, offering prayers.
That is the basic idea that how we can minimize our bodily needs and focus more on nourishing the soul, our spiritual needs.
At times, caught up in this rat race, we end up neglecting the requirements of the soul, our spiritual needs.
And that's why at times people are spiritually starved.
So now let's come to Ekadashi.
Ekadashi as the word, it's a Sanskrit word.
Ekadashi means 11.
Ekadashi, Dvaadashi, Treyodashi.
So on the 11th day, twice in a month, 11th day of the waxing moon or the rising moon.
The new moon or the waxing moon, the 11th day is called the Ekadashi.
And on Ekadashi, we follow the Vrata.
We fast.
And with the same purpose of minimizing our bodily requirement, and we can focus more on remembering the Lord, chanting more about the Lord.
Shri Prabhupada said that on Ekadashi day, it will be nice if devotees can chant 25 rounds.
Usually, he recommended chanting 16 rounds minimum.
And he recommended that how we can chant more rounds on Ekadashi.
With the same principle in mind.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, when he took sannyas, he was leaving home.
When he was parting from his mother, so he fell at her mother's feet.
And I said, I have only one request from you.
I just need one gift from you.
So imagine a mother, the son is leaving the home, is taking sannyas and going away for good.
So mother,
was overwhelmed with lot of emotions, feelings. The son is leaving home and she
said, yes, whatever you want I am willing to do for you.
So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu folded hands, pleaded her mother, please follow the Ekadashi
Vrata. He didn't ask any other thing. He didn't say you should chant the
names of the Lord. He didn't say you should read Bhagavad Gita daily. He didn't say you should read
Bhagavatam. He had only one request from the mother. And mind you, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is none other
than Lord Shri Krishna himself, who has come in this age of Kali, Kali Yuga.
So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu requested the mother, I have only one request with folded hand.
He pleaded, please follow the Ekadashi Vrata. Please do not eat grains on
Ekadashi.
We will come to Ekadashi Vrata, how we follow, what are the various levels in which this
Vrata can be followed. And he requested the mother, please do not eat grains on the day
of Ekadashi. Follow the Ekadashi Vrata. And from that day onwards, the mother accepted
the request of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and followed the Ekadashi Vrata very diligently, very sincerely.
Now, why Ekadashi? Why on Ekadashi V fast? There is a very nice pastime, a story associated with this
particular Vrata, Ekadashi Vrata. Once, Jaimini Muni, who is the disciple of Vyasadeva, Vyasadeva is the compiler of all the Vedic literatures.
So, Jaimini Muni asked Vyasadeva, I want to understand more about Ekadashi Vrata. When
did it start? How did it start? From where did this Ekadashi come about? And what is
the significance of following this Ekadashi? So, Jaimini Muni, very, very carefully, very
humbly requested Vyasadeva, I sincerely desire to know about Ekadashi, the glories of Ekadashi,
the significance of Ekadashi. How did it all start? From where did this Ekadashi came about?
Sometime it would have started. What is the benefit of fasting on Ekadashi? Please, please
enlighten me. So, this was the request. Now, when a night
is wonderful, intelligent question is asked by a disciple to a spiritual master, it is
very pleasing to him. Because it is a very relevant question, Ekadashi is a very important
day, it is important Vrata. A disciple is asking, I am very eager to know about it.
So, Vyasadeva was very pleased to hear this enquiry from his disciple Jaimini Muni. And
he said, actually, I am incompetent to explain about the glories of Ekadashi. So, I said,
Jaimini Muni, why did you ask this question? What is the significance of this Ekadashi?
Ekadashi is so glorious, fasting on Ekadashi has so much significance that only Narayana,
only Lord who is Shad Aishwarya Purnam, unlimited strength, unlimited knowledge, unlimited wealth,
unlimited fame, unlimited beauty, unlimited renunciation, only Lord can properly describe
the glories of Ekadashi. Only He can do justice if He were to describe the glories of Ekadashi.
But still, since you have asked me this question, I will try my best to share with you the benefits,
the glories of fasting on Ekadashi, Ekadashi Vrata. So then Vyasadev says that when the
Lord created this world,
as we all have heard from the Bhagavad Gita, when the living entity, Ichcha, Dvesha, Samutthena,

envy of the Lord, he fell in this material world. Which material world? Dvandamohena,
a world which is full of Dvandva, duality, heat and cold, day and night.
Fame and infamy, happiness and distress. It's a world of duality. So the living entity
came in this material world to fulfill his propensity to be the Lord, to be the enjoyer,
to be the controller, to be the center of all attraction, to be the Lord. Now, since
it is,
the living entity,
has the desire to be independent, desire to misuse his free will to be an independent

Just like embodiment of Kali Yuga is called Kali Purusha, or the embodiment of time is
called Kala, time, personification of time.

His heart melted. And from his heart came the deity of Ekadashi. And he gave the benediction that if someone follows the Ekadashi Vrata, on the day of Ekadashi, all the sinful deeds, all the sinful reactions which a person has accumulated knowingly, unknowingly, will be burned.

And such person can get freed of these sinful reactions. Mind you, when we are talking about sinful reactions will be burned, just like it is said that when we chant the names of the Lord, all the sinful reactions are burned to ashes.
But we should not take this as a license. Yes, we can go on continuing sinful activities and we can chant the names of Lord to nullify those reactions.
Yes.
Or we can go on committing sinful activities and we do Ekadashi Vrata and all the sinful reactions will be burned. No.
These are all sinful reactions for the sins we have unknowingly, unintentionally we have done in the past.
All of us if we look back in our lives many times, many of us may be eating non-veg, all kinds of nefarious things.
Now we realize that it was wrong. What about the reactions of such deeds?
So such reactions can be burned to ashes if we engage in activities like chanting the names of the Lord or following the Ekadashi Vrata.
So that we can get purified of these sinful reactions and elevate spiritually.
Unless we are purified of the sins, sinful reactions, unless we become pious, we are not qualified even to approach the Lord.
The Lord's name will not be purified.
The Lord's name will not come on our tongue.
There are many sinful people, they have no taste, they have no desire, they have no inclination to chant the names of the Lord.
Even if they have, the conditions are so tough for them, the name will not come on their tongue.
So the Lord created this deity of Ekadashi and said that follow the Ekadashi Vrata and you will be relieved of all the sinful reactions and all the sufferings.
So people started following the Ekadashi Vrata.
And all the sinful reactions started getting annihilated, destroyed.
People started becoming pious and many of them started proceeding towards Vaikuntha.
Few days later, this Papa Purusha realized that because of the influence of Ekadashi, his own influence on the people, on the living entities in general is diminishing.

They are becoming pious.
Now practically he has no work.

Less and less people are committing sins.

That Lord looks like my effect on the people in general is diminishing.

They are getting more and more purified.

have milk, you can have nuts, you can have dry fruits, but avoid eating grains. But then she
wrote back saying that because grains take little time to digest and the doctors have
recommended that we should not eat grains, we should not eat grains. So I wrote back to her
saying that because grains take little time to digest and the doctors have recommended
that we should not eat grains, we should not eat grains. So I wrote back saying that because
grains take little time to digest and the doctors have recommended that do not undertake
any kind of fasting because it will disrupt the body's systems. So, we can consult the
doctor if we are severely diabetic and the problem is quite acute, but in general, if a
depending on how severe is our medical condition we can consult our doctor take his opinion and

very difficult then better follow doctor's advice we cannot you know completely ignore the advice
of a doctor and then fast but in general if we tell the doctor that yes except for grains we

Just that in case of Tulsi plant,
because it is,
it is worshipable when we offer some Aarati or something,
we also offer some water few drops and in the temple specifically,
because all the devotees offer water doing Pradakshina,
Tulsi Pradakshina going around the circumambulating Tulsi Devi.
We just put three drops of water because too much of water can also be not good for the Tulsi plant.
So just that caution we have to exercise not overwater the plant.
And,
like any other plant we can offer water to Tulsi Devi.
This question is from Triyan Baru.
How is Shiva's way and way of Krishna different?
Shiva is also a very wonderful,
exalted personality.
He is the greatest Vaishnava.
Vaishnavanam Yatha Shambhu.
And we see the picture of Shiva,
you know,
meditating with the Rudraksha,
meditating with the Rudraksha,
he is actually meditating on Vishnu.
In fact,
somebody may wonder,
just because you are devotees of Krishna,
you are saying so?
Actually in the Srimad Bhagavatam,
which is the final work of Ved Vyas,
there is a full chapter dedicated on the prayers offered by Lord Shiva.
And in that chapter,
in that particular chapter,
Lord Shiva mentions and glorifies the position of Lord Shiva.
Lord Shiva identifies the position of Vishnu or Krishna.
And he says that I am just a servant,
I am just a devotee of such exalted personality.
So this is not being fanatical that just because we are devotees of Krishna,
therefore we say so.
There is a scriptural authority basis which we are saying that.
